Osenpa Auto Clicker is a desktop automat:
Osenpa Auto Clicker is a desktop automation tool. It lets your computer click the mouse, press keyboard keys, and find colors or images on screen — all automatically, as many times as you want.

Think of it like a robot that copies exactly what you tell it to do, and repeats it perfectly every time.
Everything you want the computer to do i:
Everything you want the computer to do is called a Step. You build a list of steps, and Osenpa Auto Clicker runs them in order.

• KEYBOARD step — presses a key (e.g. Enter, Space, A, Ctrl+C)
• MOUSE step — clicks at a position on screen
• COLOR step — waits until a color appears, then clicks it
• IMAGE step — waits until a picture appears on screen, then clicks it
• DELAY step — pauses for a moment before continuing
• IF COLOR / IF IMAGE — checks if something is there, and does one thing if yes, another if no

TIP: Double-click any step in the list to edit it. Drag steps up and down to reorder them.
Use the Keyboard panel to press any key:
Use the Keyboard panel to press any key or key combination.

• SINGLE KEY — press one key at a time (e.g. press F5 to refresh)
• HOTKEY COMBO — press multiple keys at once (e.g. ctrl+z to undo, ctrl+shift+s to save as)
• REPEAT — how many times to press the key
• INTERVAL — how long to wait between each press

TIP: Click inside the KEY box and press the key you want — it gets captured automatically. No typing required.
Use the Mouse panel to click anywhere on:
Use the Mouse panel to click anywhere on the screen.

• FIXED COORDINATES — click at a specific X,Y position on screen
• CURRENT CURSOR POSITION — click wherever your mouse is at the time
• LEFT / RIGHT / MIDDLE button — choose which mouse button to click
• DRAG — click and drag from one point to another (like drag-and-drop)

TIP: Use the PICK TARGET button to click on the screen and automatically fill in the X,Y coordinates. No manual typing!
Color Detection watches the screen and c:
Color Detection watches the screen and clicks as soon as it spots a color you have defined.

• PICK COLOR — opens a color picker to choose an exact color
• PICK FROM SCREEN — click anywhere on your screen to grab that pixel's color
• TOLERANCE — how exact the color match must be. A value of 15–30 works well for most cases. Higher value = more forgiving (catches similar shades too). Lower value = very strict exact match only.
• SCAN INTERVAL — how often to check the screen (e.g. every 0.5 seconds)
• STEP INTERVAL — how long to wait after each click before scanning again

TIP: You can add multiple colors. The detector will click when ANY of them appears on screen — whichever it finds first.
Image Detection looks for a picture (cal:
Image Detection looks for a picture (called a template) on your screen and clicks it as soon as it finds it.

• LOAD IMAGE FILE — pick a .png or .jpg file from your computer
• FROM CLIPBOARD — if you copied a screenshot, paste it directly here
• CONFIDENCE — how closely the screen must match your template image. 0.8 (80%) is a good starting point. Lower = more lenient match.

TIP: After each click, the mouse automatically moves to the corner of your screen so it does not block the image on the next scan. This is built-in.

TIP: Use a small, unique part of a button or icon as your template — not the whole screen. Smaller templates match faster and more reliably.
🔀 IF COLOR / IF IMAGE — Conditional Ste: 🔀  IF COLOR / IF IMAGE — Conditional Steps
IF steps are like a decision point in yo:
IF steps are like a decision point in your automation — imagine a fork in the road where the computer looks at the screen and chooses which path to take.

How it works:
1. The computer looks at the screen for a specific color or image
2. IF it FINDS it → it does the THEN action
3. IF it does NOT find it → it does the ELSE action

Example — IF COLOR:
• You are playing a game and you want the bot to click a green button when it appears, but skip and wait when it's not there yet.
• IF COLOR = green → THEN = click it → ELSE = do nothing

Example — IF IMAGE:
• You want to check if a 'Loading...' spinner is visible.
• IF IMAGE = spinner.png → THEN = wait 2 seconds → ELSE = continue clicking

Available actions for THEN and ELSE:
• CLICK FOUND — click exactly where the color/image was found
• CLICK (fixed position) — click a specific coordinate
• PRESS KEY — press a keyboard key
• DELAY — wait a number of seconds
• STOP — stop the automation completely
• DO NOTHING — skip and move to the next step

TIP: IF steps are great for automations that need to react differently depending on what's happening on screen — like a game that changes state, or a website that shows different buttons at different times.

TIP: You can set a TIMEOUT for IF steps. If the color/image is not found within that many seconds, the ELSE branch runs automatically.
The Macro Recorder watches everything yo:
The Macro Recorder watches everything you do on the keyboard and mouse, then converts your actions into steps automatically.

1. Open the Record Actions panel
2. Click START RECORDING (or press the hotkey)
3. Do whatever you want to automate — type, click, navigate
4. Press the hotkey again to stop recording
5. Osenpa Auto Clicker will add all your actions as steps

TIP: Record once, then run as many times as you like.
Once you have steps in your list, click:
Once you have steps in your list, click RUN STEPS at the bottom (or press F7) to start.

• LOOP — run the steps more than once
• INFINITE — keep repeating until you press STOP
• STEP INTERVAL — add extra wait time between each step
• RUN FROM HERE — use the button to start from a specific step instead of from the beginning

TIP: Press F7 to start and stop at any time, even when the app is minimized in the taskbar.
Profiles let you save your panel setting:
Profiles let you save your panel settings and reload them any time.

• Enter a name and click SAVE CURRENT to save your current settings
• Select a profile from the list and click LOAD PROFILE to restore it
• You can save settings from all panels, or just one specific panel

TIP: Create different profiles for different tasks — one for gaming, one for work, one for web automation.
The Settings panel lets you customize th:
The Settings panel lets you customize the app:

• SHOW ACTIVE TASK INDICATOR — a small overlay in the bottom-right corner shows what is running while automation is active
• SHOW SCAN AREA BORDER — highlights the region being scanned for color or image detection
• HOTKEYS — see all current keyboard shortcuts at a glance
• AUTO-CLOSE TIMER — set the app to close itself after a chosen amount of time (great for setting a session limit)
• RESET ALL — clear everything and start fresh (your saved profiles are always kept)
• Use Ctrl+Z to undo the last change to:
• Use Ctrl+Z to undo the last change to your step list. Ctrl+Y to redo.

• Add a DELAY step between fast actions to give the computer time to respond. 0.5–1 second is often enough.

• For web automation, use Image Detection to find buttons — it works even when button positions shift around.

• For game automation, Color Detection is faster and more reliable than image matching when targeting a single colored pixel or area.

• Use the PICK AREA button in Color/Image panels to limit scanning to a small part of the screen — this makes detection much faster.

• Export your steps to a JSON file to share them or back them up.

• Press F7 any time to stop an automation — even in the middle of a step.
Q: Why doesn't Color Detection find my c:
Q: Why doesn't Color Detection find my color?
A: Try raising the Tolerance value (15–30 is recommended). Lighting, screen scaling, and image compression can all shift colors slightly.

Q: Why doesn't Image Detection find my image?
A: Make sure the template looks exactly like what's on screen. Try lowering Confidence to 0.7. Use a tight crop of the unique element — avoid templates with lots of background or changing content.

Q: Can I run steps while the app is minimized?
A: Yes. Once started, automation continues in the background. Use the F7 hotkey to stop it at any time.

Q: Steps are running too fast or too slow — what do I do?
A: Adjust the INTERVAL on each individual step, or use the STEP INTERVAL in the footer to add a global delay between all steps.

Q: I accidentally deleted my steps. Can I undo?
A: Yes! Press Ctrl+Z to undo. Osenpa Auto Clicker keeps full undo history.

Q: What is IF COLOR / IF IMAGE used for?
A: They let your automation make decisions. For example: if a green button appears on screen, click it; otherwise wait. See the IF COLOR / IF IMAGE section above for full details.

Tolerance controls how strict the color:
Tolerance controls how strict the color match is.
0 = exact match only.
Higher values = allows slight shade differences.
Confidence controls how identical the im:
Confidence controls how identical the image on screen needs to be.
1.0 = exact pixel match.
0.8 = usually ignores slight color depth/rendering differences.
